title: On the Endurance of the Spirit
author: From: The Prophet by Kahlil Gibran
content: Work is love made visible. If you cannot work with love but only with distaste, it is better that you should leave your work.
action: Action: Re-orient your intent toward one project; find one aspect of it you can do with 'love' or genuine care.

title: The Law of Triviality
author: From: Parkinson's Law
content: The time spent on any item of the agenda will be in inverse proportion to the sum involved. People love debating small things because they are easy to understand.
action: Action: Identify a minor topic currently consuming time and move it to a 'quick decision' bucket.

title: The Sunk Cost Fallacy
author: From: Behavioral Economics
content: The money or time already invested is gone. Future decisions should be based only on future value.
action: Action: If you were starting your current project today, would you choose the same path? If no, change course.

title: The 10,000 Hour Myth
author: From: Range by David Epstein
content: Practice without feedback does not lead to mastery. Deliberate, difficult, and corrected practice leads to mastery.
action: Action: Add one specific 'difficulty' to your practice of a skill to ensure you are actually improving.

title: The Observer Effect
author: From: Quantum Physics
content: The act of observing a system changes the system. Your attention is not neutral; it is an active force.
action: Action: Be mindful of your presence in a team—your focus shapes their output.
